  "findings": [
    "PVC flue slope is back-pitched, which will trap acidic condensate in the venting system instead of draining it back to the furnace or condensate drain."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work on the venting system and re-slope the PVC flue piping to maintain a minimum pitch of 1/4 inch per foot back to the appliance or designated drain."
  ],
  "rationale": "A back-pitched condensing flue pipe traps condensate, which can restrict flue gas flow, cause the furnace pressure switch to trip, or lead to premature failure of the venting system and potential carbon monoxide leakage.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801",
    "NFPA 54"
  ]
}
